item.title: Fiji FACT semi-finals locked in
item.description: The semi-final line-up for the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT has been confirmed, with hosts Labasa set to face Ba in a highly anticipated clash at Subrail Park next Saturday. The two sides have developed a strong rivalry this season, with Labasa winning their only previous meeting 2-1.
item.content:encoded:
The semi-final line-up for the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT has been confirmed, with hosts Labasa set to face Ba in a highly anticipated clash at Subrail Park next Saturday.

The two sides have developed a strong rivalry this season, with Labasa winning their only previous meeting 2-1.

A large number of Ba supporters are expected to travel north for the match. In the second semi-final, Lautoka will take on Navua in another closely contested fixture.

Ba and Labasa kick off at 2pm, followed by Lautoka versus Navua at 4.30pm. The final will be played on Sunday at 3pm.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Fiji FACT finals at Subrail Park
item.description: The semi-finals and final of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT will be played at Labasa’s Subrail Park. This has been confirmed by Fiji FA CEO Mohammed Yusuf.
item.content:encoded:
The semi-finals and final of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT will be played at Labasa’s Subrail Park.

This has been confirmed by Fiji FA CEO Mohammed Yusuf.

The first semi-final will see hosts Labasa take on Ba at 2pm on Saturday while Navua will play either Lautoka or Nadroga in the second semi-final at 4.30pm.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Labasa knock Rewa out of Fiji FACT
item.description: The Labasa football side beat Rewa 4-2 to top Group A at the BiC Fiji FACT underway at Subrail Park in Labasa. Backed by vocal fans, Labasa needed a victory to advance while Rewa just needed a draw.
item.content:encoded:
The Labasa football side beat Rewa 4-2 to top Group A at the BiC Fiji FACT underway at Subrail Park in Labasa.

Backed by vocal fans, Labasa needed a victory to advance while Rewa just needed a draw.

Jovesa Uqe, Ashneel Raju and Lekima Gonerau scored first half goals for Labasa as they led 3-2 at halftime. Semi Nabenu scored for Rewa while Labasa conceded an own goal in the first spell.

Jone Lalagavesi got the lone goal of the second spell to seal the win for Labasa. Rewa bow out of the tournament after the loss.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Navua secures semi-final spot
item.description: The Navua football side has secured a spot in the semi-finals of the BiC Fiji FACT after a convincing 2-nil win over Suva in their last pool match today. Junior Jack and Bati Tito scored on either side of the break to secure the win and a spot in the last four next week.
item.content:encoded:
The Navua football side has secured a spot in the semi-finals of the BiC Fiji FACT after a convincing 2-nil win over Suva in their last pool match today.

Junior Jack and Bati Tito scored on either side of the break to secure the win and a spot in the last four next week.

Navua is expected to face Lautoka or Nadroga in the semi-finals next week.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Rugby club continues strong campaign
item.description: The Qionimaravu rugby team has continued their dominant run at the Naitasiri Rugby Union club games with a commanding 25-3 victory over Flametree Colo-i-Suva yesterday at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori. Taking a commanding 15-0 lead at halftime, Qionimaravu set the tone early.
item.content:encoded:
The Qionimaravu rugby team has continued their dominant run at the Naitasiri Rugby Union club games with a commanding 25-3 victory over Flametree Colo-i-Suva yesterday at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori.

Taking a commanding 15-0 lead at halftime, Qionimaravu set the tone early.

Despite a spirited second-half effort from Colo-i-Suva, the team maintained their composure, shutting down any hope of a comeback from their opponents.

Captain Josaia Navualiku credited their intense preparation during the week for the resounding win.

“We really worked hard on our mistakes from our last game and that has paid dividends in our victory today (yesterday),” he said.

Team Manager Joasaia Rakavono praised the squad’s resilience, especially given their challenging circumstances.

“We are one of the teams who are based up at the highlands,” he explained.

“Despite the cold, our long travels every week, the boys continue to perform consistently each week which shows their pride in representing the team and their village.”

Looking ahead, the team is focussing on sharpening discipline and their forwards’ game as they prepare for their next outing.

With such determination and unity, Qionimaravu looks poised to maintain their strong campaign in the competition.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Hard work pays off
item.description: McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women captain Kolara Lomani believes her side’s improved start laid the foundation for their impressive 42-25 victory over the Brumbies in their Super W clash at 4R Stadium yesterday. After an inconsistent opening performance in the previous round, Lomani said the team made it a priority to improve the opening stages of the match.
item.content:encoded:
McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women captain Kolara Lomani believes her side’s improved start laid the foundation for their impressive 42-25 victory over the Brumbies in their Super W clash at 4R Stadium yesterday.

After an inconsistent opening performance in the previous round, Lomani said the team made it a priority to improve the opening stages of the match.

“We worked on what we missed out on last week, which was our first 10 to 20 minutes of the game, especially our physicality and collision work,” she said.

“The girls brought that into the game. We went into halftime, regrouped and came back stronger in the second half.”

The Drua held their nerve despite the Brumbies fighting back to level the scores at halftime and threatening again in the second spell.

Lomani said the team’s composure reflected the character and growing maturity within the squad.

“We always have respect for the Brumbies girls because they always bring a good fight. Our girls stayed in the contest and played right through the full 80 minutes.”

The victory gives the defending champions valuable momentum heading into their final regular-season match against the Reds next week.

Lomani said the players understood the importance of securing the win.

“We locked in during the week because this game was very important for us. Winning gives us confidence and momentum heading into our last game against the Reds next week.”

The Drua will now turn their attention to next week’s crucial clash as they continue their push in the Super W competition.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Sharks begin title defence
item.description: Police Sharks women’s captain Laisa Takayawa says the defending champions were ready to begin their title defense as the Vodafone Fiji Cup women’s competition began yesterday. The 2026 Vodafone Fiji Cup was officially launched on Thursday, with round one of matches getting underway as clubs across the country launch their campaigns for national honours.
item.content:encoded:
Police Sharks women’s captain Laisa Takayawa says the defending champions were ready to begin their title defense as the Vodafone Fiji Cup women’s competition began yesterday.

The 2026 Vodafone Fiji Cup was officially launched on Thursday, with round one of matches getting underway as clubs across the country launch their campaigns for national honours.

Speaking during the launch, Takayawa said the team is well prepared for the opening round but expects a much tougher season ahead as all teams raise their standard.

“We are well prepared and looking forward to our first game,” she said.

“We won’t take any team lightly this year.”

The Police Sharks have dominated the women’s competition for the past two seasons, but Takayawa acknowledged that retaining the title will not be easy following the loss of several players who have secured overseas contracts.

Despite the changes in personnel, Takayawa said the team’s foundation remains built on discipline, teamwork, and respect for opponents.

The Police Sharks met the Serua Dragons yesterday.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: ‘Every lap was worth it’: swim captain
item.description: While the medals, trophies and championship banners reflect Drasa Avenue Primary School’s dominance in the pool, team captain and deputy head girl Samoana Powell believes their success was built long before race day. Standing before fellow students, officials and supporters, Powell delivered an emotional speech that paid tribute to everyone who helped the girls secure a fourth consecutive National
item.content:encoded:
While the medals, trophies and championship banners reflect Drasa Avenue Primary School’s dominance in the pool, team captain and deputy head girl Samoana Powell believes their success was built long before race day.

Standing before fellow students, officials and supporters, Powell delivered an emotional speech that paid tribute to everyone who helped the girls secure a fourth consecutive National Schools Swimming Championship title.

Rather than focusing on personal achievements, the young leader chose to shine the spotlight on the people behind the success.

“First and foremost, we give all glory and praise to God for blessing us with the strength, determination and endurance to swim our very best throughout the season,” she said.

She recalled the sacrifices made by the team during months of preparation, from early morning training sessions to the countless laps completed when exhaustion threatened to take over.

“There were times we honestly wondered if Coach was preparing us for the Olympics.

Powell praised praise for head coach Salaseini Lele, crediting her unwavering belief and demanding training sessions for helping transform the swimmers into champions.

“Looking back now, we realise every lap, every drill and every early morning training session was worth it.”

She also acknowledged the many people who worked quietly behind the scenes, including volunteers, team supporters, school management, teachers and donors who ensured the swimmers had everything they needed throughout the season.

For Powell, the biggest appreciation belonged to the parents and guardians.

She thanked them for waking before sunrise, ensuring swimmers reached training by 6am with their gear packed and for cheering loudly from the stands during competitions.

“Whether we won, lost or swallowed half the pool water, you were always there supporting us,” she said.

Powell also recognised sponsors whose generosity helped ease the financial burden of competing.

She thanked the school’s head, Master Sharma, for his leadership and encouragement, saying his presence at the National Schools Swimming Championship boosted the team’s confidence.

The captain also paid tribute to her teammates, describing them as her second family.

Her heartfelt speech reflected the values synonymous with the Super Dolphers programme: faith, discipline, teamwork and gratitude.

As the school celebrated another historic championship, Powell reminded everyone that while medals are won in the pool, true champions are shaped by the people who stand beside them every step of the journey.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
